title: Olanzapine
content_markdown:
##### Type
Antipsychotic

- **High-risk medication**

##### Presentations
5mg and 10mg wafer 

##### Actions
Olanzapine is an atypical antipsychotic. 
 Acts on several neurotransmitters; antipsychotic actions are thought to be mediated by dopamine blockade. 
 Onset of action: PO 10-20 min | Duration 12-24 hours

#### Uses
Pharmacological intervention for mild to moderately agitated patients.

#### Adverse Effects

- Prolonged QT interval (particularly in the elderly)

- Extrapyramidal effects (rare)

- Neuroleptic malignant syndrome (rare)

- May lower seizure threshold

#### Contraindications

- Known hypersensitivity

- Parkinson’s disease

- Post ictal

#### Precautions
Increased effects in patients who have consumed alcohol, recreational drugs and with other sedative agents.

#### Pregnancy and Breastfeeding##### Pregnancy
- **Contraindicated** when 14 years)
- 10mg wafer 
- Repeat once, after 20 minutes, if required.

- Modify dose to 5mg wafer if: 
- Frail/elderly (>65 years) or
- Aboriginal/Torres Straight Island background >55 years- Repeat once, after 10-15 minutes, if required.##### Paediatric (
